---
name: Good first issue
about: A scoped task suitable for a first-time contributor (maintainers only)
title: '[GOOD FIRST ISSUE] '
labels: good-first-issue, help-wanted
assignees: ''
---

> Use this template only when filing an issue you've already scoped well enough that someone new to the project can pick it up. The point of `good-first-issue` is that the path from "I want to help" to "PR open" is short and unambiguous.

## What needs to be done

A clear, concrete description of the task.

## Why this is a good first issue

What makes this approachable? What part of the codebase will the contributor learn? What does the contributor get to keep after shipping it?

## Where to look

- File(s) involved: `src/...`
- Related design doc: `docs/...` (if any)
- Reference / inspiration: (e.g. "see how X works in `src/...`")

## How to verify it works

The acceptance criteria. The contributor should be able to read this and know when they're done.

- [ ] Criterion 1
- [ ] Criterion 2
- [ ] (etc.)

## Estimated size

Choose one: **tiny** (<1 hour), **small** (1–4 hours), **medium** (a day), **large** (a week — usually too big for a first issue, consider splitting).

## Mentor

A maintainer willing to answer questions during implementation. (Default: whoever opens this issue.)
